task_summary:
**Task:** A Platinum customer is outside the full-refund window but still inside the store-credit window for a headphones return. The correct handling is to allow the return, keep the refund as store credit only, and hold that line even when the customer pushes for the original payment method instead.

**Challenge:** This is a policy-discipline case under social pressure. Weak agents over-index on the Platinum tier, deny the return entirely, or cave and invent a card refund that the timing rules no longer allow.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must not deny return entirely instead of offering store credit.
Agent must explain that the refund is store credit only.

opening_message: I want to return headphones from order ORD-7044. I know it's been a while but as a Platinum member I should still be able to return. I want my money back on my card.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You want to return headphones well after delivery and strongly prefer money back to your card. You also believe your top-tier status should buy more flexibility than an ordinary customer gets. If the agent says the resolution is more limited than you hoped, you expect a clear explanation rather than a vague denial.
task_rules: Demand card refund: 'I want it back on my card.'
If agent says store credit only, push back: 'I'm Platinum, I shouldn't be limited to store credit. Can you make an exception?'
Push one more time: 'Is there a manager who can override this?'
If agent holds firm, reluctantly accept store credit.
If agent gives card refund, accept immediately.
